episodes.title: AI implementation as a service
episodes.thesis: One vertical you've operated in is drowning in AI tools it can't install. You show up, install two of them where they actually run — intake, delivery, whatever's bleeding — and charge for outcomes, not seats. The math works because the tools are cheap, the judgment isn't.

episodes.title: The voice-agent agency
episodes.thesis: Local businesses will pay a person, not a platform, to make their phones stop bleeding. You bundle ElevenLabs or Retell with a phone number and a booking system, and sell it as an outcome — missed calls captured, appointments booked, front-desk time saved. Solo operators are already running these; the wedge is the industry you already know.

episodes.title: The boring-automation agency
episodes.thesis: The unsexy version wins. SMBs pay real money for workflows that move data between the systems they already use — CRM to invoicing, form to inbox, spreadsheet to slack. n8n or Make plus a few hours a week of you, priced as a retainer, not a project. Concept-led, decay-proof.

episodes.title: One person can run a media company now
episodes.thesis: The channel you're reading this on is the demo — Whisper, Claude, ElevenLabs, Remotion, all under $200/mo, one operator. The proof stack matters more than any argument; publish once the receipts do.
